report_sections.section_text: the waking background shows good organization, consisting of predominantly  30-40 uv, 9 hz symmetric posterior background activity with good reactivity.  there is moderate beta activity bilaterally. intermittent drowsiness is  characterized by attenuation of the background, slow roving eye movements and  bilateral slowing in the theta and delta range with shifting predominance.  stage n2 sleep is characterized by, further slowing, vertex sharp waves,  sleep spindles, and k-complexes.   hyperventilation for 3 minutes results in mild bilateral drive. photic  stimulation results in moderate bilateral driving.   single lead ekg shows an apparent sinus rhythm of approximately 70 beats per  minute.    this routine eeg, performed in the waking, drowsy and  sleeping states is within normal limits. there are no definite focal  abnormalities. there are no prior eegs available for comparison.
